title: Trust-card and certification decisions are bound to verified evidence

obligations.description: Inline unit coverage proves trust-card create/update fail closed without verified evidence refs, stores derivation metadata when evidence exists, and certification preserves a derivation chain only for evidence-backed decisions.
Conformance coverage exercises the artifact-admission to trust-card boundary, including fail-closed trust-card creation when upstream contract evidence is removed.
Mock-free lifecycle coverage drives create, read, upgrade, revoke, and snapshot paths with real verified evidence refs and rejects missing-evidence creation and unevidenced certification upgrades.

trust_cards: TrustCardRegistry::create rejects empty evidence refs and stores DerivationMetadata.
TrustCardRegistry::update rejects certification upgrades without evidence refs.
Provided update evidence replaces the trust-card derivation chain.
certification: CertificationResult carries DerivationMetadata for evidence-backed evaluations.
Certification without evidence returns Uncertified and records evidence_binding_present as unsatisfied.
Domain-separated derivation hashes include evidence ids, evidence types, epochs, and receipt hashes.

validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenengine-node --test e2e_trust_card_lifecycle
validation.actual: SKIPPED at closeout: pgrep -af 'cargo|rustc' | wc -l returned 5; rch queue reported 3 active builds

notes: bd-1oju already implemented the verified-evidence binding in the Rust trust-card and certification modules.
bd-1oju.1 closes the audit-recorded completion-debt items by making the trust-card checker ratchet the implementation, unit, integration, E2E, and evidence-pack coverage.
The checker now fails closed if this completion-debt evidence pack, its summary, or any cited evidence path is missing.
